title: caladenia tonellii
intro: Caladenia tonellii, also known as the Donkey Orchid, is a species of orchid native to Western Australia, characterized by its distinctive donkey-like labellum and delicate white or yellowish-green flowers. The plant is rich in flavonoids, particularly naringenin and quercetin, which are known for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. Caladenia tonellii has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, respiratory problems, and skin conditions, due to its ability to stimulate the immune system and promote wound healing. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
